svg.text: HAWAIIAN + ENGLISH — RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
ALT DRAFT · SERIES 59D · REMEMBER FORWARD
— I. HAWAIIAN — ʻALEKOKI (NUUANU PALI LOOKOUT PASSAGE) —
I nā mea ʻino o ka ʻāina,
e hoʻomanaʻo i nā kupuna.
In the evil-times of the land / remember the ancestors.
"In difficult times for the land, remember the ancestors." (Hawaiian teaching, oral tradition)
— II. QUEEN LILIʻUOKALANI — ʻALOHA ʻOE (1878 CE) —
ʻAloha ʻoe, ʻaloha ʻoe,
E ke onaona noho i ka lipo,
Farewell to you farewell to you / The gentle breezes blowing to the deep.
"Aloha ʻOe" — composed by Queen Liliʻuokalani, last monarch of Hawaiiʻi (deposed 1893).
— III. HAWAIIAN BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
Ua hana ʻia kēia no ʻoukou, me ka lokomaikaʻi,
e nā kanaka nāna i hoʻomanaʻo i nā wā e hiki mai ana.
PAST make PASS this for you with the generosity / by the people who remembered the era coming.
"This was made for you, generously, by people who remembered the time to come." (Hawaiian)
— HAWAIIAN NOTE — “FUTURE” AND “REMEMBER” —
Nā wā e hiki mai ana = the times that are coming (no single word for “future” — a temporal phrase).
Hoʻomanaʻo = to remember, to commemorate. Hoʻo- = causative prefix + manaʻo (thought/intention).
Lokomaikaʻi = generosity, kindness (loko = inside + maikaʻi = good). Free giving from the inside.
SECONDARY — ENGLISH — RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
— I. EMILY DICKINSON — ON MEMORY (c.1863 CE) —
The Brain — is wider than the Sky —
For — put them side by side — / The one the other will contain.
"The brain is wider than the sky." — Emily Dickinson. On the interior infinity of memory and imagination.
— II. ENGLISH PROVERB — ON TIME —
The present moment always will have been.
"The present moment always will have been." — The permanence of past moments. Core Remember Forward concept.
— III. ENGLISH BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
This was made for you, freely,
by people who remembered forward.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered forward." (English — the project’s own language)
— ENGLISH NOTE — “FORWARD” —
“Remember forward”: an English oxymoron. You cannot remember what has not happened.
Yet: you can act now as if the future were already a past worth honoring. That is the project’s name.
“Freely”: from Old English freodom. Gift-giving without expectation of return. The grammar of generosity.
— LANGUAGE BRIDGE — AN OCEAN APART, FOREVER ENTANGLED —
Hawaiian: ~2000 native speakers (critically endangered, revitalizing). Polynesian origin, 1000+ years on islands.
English: ~380M native speakers. Global lingua franca. The language of the colonization that endangered Hawaiian.
Today English is also the medium through which Hawaiian revitalization reaches the world. Complicated neighbors.
PLATE 59D ALT · Hawaiian / English · RUNNING TEXT · CC BY-SA 4.0 · REMEMBERFORWARD.ORG
THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
